domain_knowledge: The adaptive_coarsegrain utility is a library-level function introduced in v0.3.0 alongside other plotting and utility modules for Hi-C analysis support.
Library utilities in cooltools are organized under the cooltools.lib subpackage and must be documented in docs/api.rst following the Sphinx Autosummary extension convention.
Code must follow PEP-8 style standards and pass flake8 linting; pytest-flake8 automatically enforces this during test execution.
Functions must include Numpy-style docstrings to be properly indexed by Sphinx and appear in the generated API reference documentation.
Editable installation mode (pip install -e .) creates a symbolic link allowing live code changes to be immediately reflected without reinstallation.

expert_review: confirm that adaptive_coarsegrain is semantically consistent with described functionality as a library utility for coarse-graining operations
verify that the function's implementation and documentation align with standard Hi-C analysis practices for adaptive resolution adjustment

workflow_description: 1. Clone the cooltools repository from open2c/cooltools. 2. Install the package in editable development mode using pip install -e . 3. Import the adaptive_coarsegrain function from cooltools.lib and confirm it is callable. 4. Run pytest on the lib package unit tests to verify function behavior and code coverage are satisfactory. 5. Build the Sphinx documentation using make docs and verify the function appears in the cooltools.lib API reference. 6. Generate a test report confirming successful import, execution, and documentation inclusion.
